model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Arizona is the stronger team by a clear margin in the available power ratings, sitting #3 in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite defense (top-3 on both) and a top-10 offense, while Arkansas profiles more like a top-20 team with a much more vulnerable defense (around #50). Arkansas’ path is credible because its offense is truly elite (top-5 AdjO) and can win a one-game shot-making battle, but Arizona’s defensive ceiling plus overall efficiency edge gives it more reliable ways to control the game. With no verified, matchup-flipping availability issue surfaced in the quick injury/news checks, I’m siding with the higher two-way baseline.

reasoningStep.title: Sweet 16 (West) — Arizona vs Arkansas
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona advances based on a sizable overall efficiency edge and a much stronger defensive profile, while acknowledging Arkansas’ elite offense makes the upset plausible.
evidence: Ratings snapshot: Arizona (KenPom #3, AdjEM 37.56; AdjD 89.7) vs Arkansas (KenPom #17, AdjEM 25.43; AdjD 102.1).
Torvik snapshot: Arizona #3 (Def rank 3) vs Arkansas #20 (Def rank 50), with both offenses ranking top-10/top-5 respectively.
Injury/news scan via web/ESPN search did not surface a specific confirmed availability issue for either team that would materially change the baseline matchup.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Arizona (arizona) vs Arkansas (arkansas)
Prior committed picks: 54
